implementedFeatures: Application lifecycle state machine with 10 statuses and explicit transition guards (server/src/api/applications/applications.service.ts)
Offer-condition evaluation with deterministic auto-promotion via evaluateOfferConditionsAndAutoPromote() and fail-soft safeEvaluateOfferConditions() wrapper
POST /v1/applications/:id/convert with Person→Student→Enrolment idempotency (re-runs return existing IDs and isNewStudent/isNewEnrolment flags)

POST /v1/marks/aggregate with deterministic weighted-average aggregation (Phase 17A): pure utility in server/src/utils/marks-aggregation.ts handles two-decimal rounding, non-positive maxMark/weighting guards, and explicit missing-component reporting; service orchestrator routes persistence through module-results.service so its existing audit/events fire normally; refuses to overwrite CONFIRMED ModuleResult; rejects incomplete or non-100%-weighted aggregations without force=true; emits marks.aggregated on its own webhook path

Moderation / ratification action endpoints (Phase 17B): POST /v1/marks/:id/moderate drives MARKED→MODERATED with required moderatedMark, moderator-marker independence guard, and moderatedDate/moderatedBy auto-stamping; POST /v1/marks/:id/ratify drives MODERATED→CONFIRMED with finalMark auto-derivation (moderatedMark fallback to rawMark) and rejects re-ratifying a CONFIRMED row; POST /v1/module-results/:id/ratify drives PROVISIONAL→CONFIRMED with the existing assertAllAttemptsConfirmed cross-entity guard plus confirmedDate/confirmedBy auto-stamping; all three action endpoints route through the canonical update() flow so audit and existing events (marks.status_changed, marks.ratified, marks.released, module_results.status_changed, module_results.ratified) fire on their normal paths

Cohort-level module result generation (Phase 17C): POST /v1/module-results/generate batches the Phase 17A aggregate primitive across every active (REGISTERED/COMPLETED) ModuleRegistration on a given (moduleId, academicYear) cohort; new repo helper moduleRegistration.repository.ts::findActiveForCohort returns the minimum {id, enrolmentId, status} projection; service marks.service.ts::generateModuleResultsForCohort orchestrates per-row aggregation with idempotency (rows whose existing ModuleResult is CONFIRMED are skipped) and per-row error isolation (one bad row does not abort the cohort); returns a summary {total, persisted, previewed, skipped, failed, results[]} so operators see the real picture; emits module_results.batch_generated on its own webhook path with summary stats; cohort-level audit subject is the Module entity rather than individual rows

Progression decisioning and degree classification (Phase 17D): pure utilities in server/src/utils/progression-decision.ts and server/src/utils/award-classification.ts encode standard UK HE rules — credit-weighted average, optional compensation envelope, REPEAT_MODULES vs REPEAT_YEAR vs WITHDRAW thresholds, AWARD on final-year completion, honours/PG/sub-honours classification boundaries — with all defaults overridable per-call so a future batch can wire ProgressionRule / ClassificationRule rows from the database without changing the utility contracts; service orchestrators progressions.service.decideForEnrolmentYear and awards.service.classifyForEnrolment load year/all ModuleResults via the new repo helpers moduleResult.repository.findForEnrolmentYear and findForEnrolment, derive isPass from CONFIRMED+aggregateMark>=passMark or CONFIRMED+passing-grade fallback, optionally upsert ProgressionRecord (idempotency lookup findByEnrolmentAndYear) or AwardRecord (idempotency lookup findByEnrolment); new POST /v1/progressions/decide and POST /v1/awards/classify endpoints REGISTRY-role gated; emits progressions.decided and awards.classified on their own webhook paths; LEVEL_8 (doctoral) is refused with an explicit reason since classification by weighted average is not appropriate at that level

Transcript composition and portal reflection (Phase 17E): pure utility server/src/utils/transcript-composition.ts orders module results by year (descending) then module code, computes per-year and final credit-weighted averages, and refuses to mark a FINAL transcript as final without a non-revoked AwardRecord; service orchestrator transcripts.service.composeForStudent loads the student, the active enrolment, all CONFIRMED ModuleResults via the 17D findForEnrolment helper, and the optional AwardRecord, then optionally writes a Transcript + TranscriptLine row set atomically through the new repository helper transcript.repository.createWithLines so the transcript is never persisted without its body; new POST /v1/transcripts/compose endpoint REGISTRY-role gated with Zod-validated body; GET /v1/transcripts widened from REGISTRY-only to ADMIN_STAFF + TEACHING + STUDENTS with scopeToUser('studentId') so the student portal can render the authenticated student's own transcripts; new MyTranscript.tsx page in the student portal at /student/transcript fetches the most recent transcript, renders its lines grouped by academic year with credit-and-mark summary stats; emits transcripts.composed event on its own webhook path with the structured payload

Fee calculation engine (Phase 18A): pure utility server/src/utils/fee-calculation.ts::calculateFee implements the canonical UK HE rule baseFee = perCreditRate(feeStatus, programmeLevel) × creditsTaken; totalFee = round(baseFee × modeMultiplier, 2); discount = min(bursaryTotal + sponsorTotal, totalFee × maxDiscountRatio); finalFee = round(max(0, totalFee − discount), 2). Default 2025/26 UK HE tariffs (HOME UG £77/credit, HOME PGT £108, HOME PGR £75; OVERSEAS UG £146, PGT £200, PGR £180; EU_TRANSITIONAL≡OVERSEAS; ISLANDS/CHANNEL_ISLANDS≡HOME) and mode multipliers (FULL_TIME 1.0, PART_TIME 0.5, SANDWICH 1.0, DISTANCE 0.8, BLOCK_RELEASE 0.6) are overridable per call. New repos feeAssessment.repository.ts (CRUD + findLatestByEnrolment for idempotency upsert), sponsorAgreement.repository.ts (findActiveByStudentYear), bursaryApplication.repository.ts (findAwardedByStudent filters APPROVED/PAID). New service feeAssessments.service.ts::assessForEnrolment orchestrates the calculation, refuses zero-totalFee persist without force=true, idempotent re-run via findLatestByEnrolment upsert (with force=true creating brand-new historical row). New endpoints POST /v1/fee-assessments/assess plus standard CRUD (GET, GET /:id, POST, PATCH /:id), all FINANCE-role gated. New dedicated webhook routes fee_assessment.created / fee_assessment.updated / fee_assessment.calculated.

Invoice and charge generation (Phase 18B): pure utility server/src/utils/invoice-composition.ts::composeInvoiceFromAssessment transforms a FeeAssessment outcome into a structured invoice body (header + chargeLines) with sensible UK HE defaults overridable per call: single TUITION line for finalFee (post-discount amount the student actually owes), 30-day default due window from issueDate, GBP currency, deterministic invoice number INV-{shortYear}-{acc8}-{fa8} so the persistence layer can resolve idempotency without a schema migration. Notes block records bursary/sponsor references and the gross→net audit trail for accounting. New repo invoice.repository.ts (CRUD + findByInvoiceNumber for idempotency lookup + createWithLines atomic Invoice + ChargeLine + StudentAccount.balance + totalDebits update in a single Prisma transaction). New helper finance.repository.findByStudentAndYear lifts the existing balance-only projection to a full StudentAccount lookup so the invoice generator can resolve the account in one round-trip. New service invoices.service.ts::generateForFeeAssessment orchestrates compose + persist with deterministic-invoice-number idempotency (existing rows return skipped:true), force:true bypasses with a -R{n} replacement counter that walks forward to find a free slot. New endpoint POST /v1/invoices/generate plus standard CRUD (GET, GET /:id, POST, PATCH /:id, DELETE /:id), all FINANCE-role gated (DELETE is SUPER_ADMIN). New dedicated webhook routes invoice.created / invoice.updated / invoice.generated / invoice.status_changed / invoice.deleted. expectedRouters bumped from 45 → 46.

Payment allocation and account-balance logic (Phase 18C): pure utility server/src/utils/payment-allocation.ts::allocatePayment distributes a Payment amount across open ChargeLines on the same StudentAccount with two strategies — FIFO (default; walks charges by dueDate then createdAt then id, fully covering each until the payment is exhausted) and PROPORTIONAL (distributes pro rata against each charge's outstanding amount with rounding-drift absorption on the last allocation). Pure: no Prisma, no I/O — same purity contract as 17A/17D/17E/18A/18B utilities. Returns a structured outcome (allocations per charge with fullyCovered flags, totalAllocated, leftover, fullyAllocated, invoiceImpact aggregated by invoiceId, operator notes) that the I/O orchestrator uses to drive mutations. New repos payment.repository.ts (CRUD with soft-delete) and chargeLine.repository.ts (read-mostly: findOpenForAccount returns non-deleted PENDING/INVOICED charges joined to non-soft-deleted invoices; markPaidBulk transaction-aware bulk status flip). New transaction-aware repo helpers invoice.repository.incrementPaidAmountInTx / findStatusProjectionInTx / updateStatusInTx and finance.repository.recordPaymentLedgerEntryInTx so the service stays repository-routed (Gate 4). New service utility utils/prisma-tx.ts::runInTransaction wraps prisma.$transaction so services can open transactions without importing the Prisma client. New service payments.service.ts::allocateForPayment orchestrates allocate + persist atomically: marks fully-covered ChargeLines as PAID, increments Invoice.paidAmount per affected invoice, promotes invoice status to PAID / PARTIALLY_PAID based on post-increment paidAmount-vs-totalAmount, decrements StudentAccount.balance and increments totalCredits by the full payment amount (leftover sits as a credit balance). Refuses to allocate non-COMPLETED payments without force=true. New endpoints POST /v1/payments plus standard CRUD plus POST /v1/payments/:id/allocate, all FINANCE-role gated (DELETE is SUPER_ADMIN). New dedicated webhook routes payment.created / payment.updated / payment.allocated / payment.status_changed / payment.deleted. expectedRouters bumped from 46 → 47.

Admissions ApplicationDetail UI exposes Convert to Student affordance (Batch 16E): button is gated on FIRM or UNCONDITIONAL_OFFER status; dialog collects yearOfStudy, modeOfStudy, startDate, feeStatus, originalEntryDate; success surface shows whether the Student/Enrolment were newly created or reused, with deep links to /admin/students/:id and /admin/enrolments/:id
37 named role exports in server/src/constants/roles.ts (36 user-facing roles + 1 ROLE_GROUPS bundle export)
47 API routers with requireRole + validate middleware on every mutating route
49 service files routed through server/src/repositories/* (no direct prisma client usage in API)
All mutations call logAudit(entityType, id, action, userId, before, after, req) and emitEvent({event, entityType, entityId, actorId, data})
EVENT_ROUTES per-event webhook path map in server/src/utils/webhooks.ts (one path per workflow, prefix fallback retained)
Request-ID correlation middleware (UUID v4, x-request-id header, Winston :reqid token)
Content-Security-Policy header in docker/nginx/nginx.prod.conf
Prisma migrations active (no db push in production phases)
Redis-backed rate limiting + helmet + CORS origin restriction
Prometheus /metrics endpoint with HTTP duration + request counter
Swagger UI at /api/docs
ESLint v9 flat config in both workspaces (advisory CI gate)
CodeQL security-extended workflow (PR/push/weekly)
Dependabot npm + github-actions
scripts/check-docs-truth.mjs as a hard CI gate against documented count drift
GitGuardian secret scanning
CODEOWNERS coverage of governance docs, workflows, auth surface, schema, nginx
SECURITY.md disclosure policy with GitHub PVR as preferred channel
partialFeatures: Attendance threshold backstop: emits AttendanceAlert + UkviBreach events but the original wire-into-pipeline TODO comment at attendance.service.ts:252 has not been deleted
Communications delivery: persists Communication records and emits events; actual SMTP/SMS dispatch is deferred to Phase 20

Marks aggregation grade resolution: only resolved when caller supplies a boundaryAssessmentId; module-level grade boundaries do not exist in the schema yet — full classification logic deferred to Phase 17D

Moderation workflow surface beyond the state machine: SecondMarkingRecord and AnonymousMarking models exist in the schema but have no service/API surface yet — second-marker reconciliation and anonymous-marker reveal flows are sequenced to a later 17B follow-on or 17C
ESLint: configs live, advisory CI job runs, but baseline violations are not yet triaged and the ratchet to blocking is tracked under KI-P15-002
npm audit baseline not yet triaged (KI-P15-001)
deferredFeatures: MFA enforcement in Keycloak realm — Phase 15B (STOP-gated; design doc required first)
Teaching-assignment model for academic scoping (KI-P10b-003) — Phase 21
Finance sub-domains: Sponsors, Bursaries, Refunds (KI-P10b-001) — Phase 18
Multi-tenancy substrate — after Phase 23 unless commercial demand pulls it forward
Activation of the 15 n8n workflows under a live n8n instance — Phase 20
Integration CI workflow that brings up docker-compose Postgres/Redis/MinIO — sequenced to Phase 20 (the docker-compose stack is not part of the GitHub runner image)

verificationGates.Gate5_serverVitest: passing (560/560 on this branch — was 504/504 after Phase 18C; Phase 18D adds 20 payment-plan-schedule pure-function cases, 13 payment-plans-service orchestration cases, and 17 payment-instalments-service cases across 3 new test files)
verificationGates.Gate10_coveragePolicy: passing (35/16/33/35 floor enforced in server/vitest.config.ts; ratchet landed in Phase 17F closeout, KI-P14-002 closed; coverage actuals on 18A are Statements 39.96%, Branches 38.18%, Functions 20.00%, Lines 39.23%, all clear the floor)
